body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
When we updated to Oceananigans v0.102, CMIP started running out of parameter memory on the P100s (i.e. in our regular CI). ~~By default Oceananigans uses Float64, so if we use Float32 it might have enough space. The configs for CMIP runs in regular CI already specify Float32.~~

Running with Float32 led to different errors (see "error links" below). It's a little weird that Oceananigans uses Float64 even when other components use Float64, but my priority right now is to be able to run CMIP in CI so I think this is fine for now.

The combination of changes that fixed the parameter memory is:
- remove eddy closure
- add order 2 buffer scheme for tracer advection (not sure what it was before)
- use Float32 for CATKE parameters

At one day, the coupler fields look very similar to how they did . The main difference is that momentum fluxes are now larger; extrema changed from ~7e-2 to ~2e-1.

## To-do
- [x] update OceananigansSimulation constructor to take in FT
- [x] set Oceananigans float type to Float64 (except for CATKE parameters)
- [x] ocean sim changes: remove eddy closure, add order 2 buffer scheme for tracer advection, use Float32 for CATKE parameters
- [x] add CMIP runs back to buildkite

## Error links
- Initial error using Oceananigans 0.102 with Float64 (CUDA parameter memory) [build](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-ci/builds/7674#019be773-5994-45fd-a371-b460e4815c2f)
- Using Float32 (CUDA illegal memory access error) [build](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-ci/builds/7696#_)
- Using Float32 + eddy closure removed (error in plotting of oceananigans sim - NaNs in simulation) [build](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-ci/builds/7710#019bf249-6a7e-4632-8a0f-9d1bea009429)
- Using Float64 + eddy closure removed (CUDA parameter memory error) [build](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-ci/builds/7711)
- Using Float64 + eddy closure removed + Float32 for CATKE parameters + buffer scheme for tracer advection (passes) [build](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-ci/builds/7714)
title: pass FT to OceananigansSimulation; change ocean sim

title: Update to ClimaOcean v0.9.0

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
To clean up setup_run.jl and prepare for moving component model code into extensions.

This shouldn't be behavior changing and it isn't for AMIP or aquaplanet, but slabplanet, terraplanet, and CMIP look slightly different, mostly in turbulent fluxes. I can't figure out why this happens, but since AMIP doesn't change I think it's okay.

Part of #1682

## Simulation type component corrections
Before, for aquaplanet and terraplanet we would construct both an ocean and a land model, and just mask out the one that shouldn't be there using the area fractions. Now, we only construct the surfaces that should be included for each simulation type (so only slab ocean for aquaplanet, and only land for terraplanet).

## To-do
- [x] make all constructors use kwargs 
- [x] add default constructors (`LandSimulation`, `OceanSimulation`, etc) for component models to extend
- [x] handle `mode_name` / model type alignment in arg parsing so we don't need to consider it in the driver
- [x] check sim modes have correct components
- [x] check results: AMIP and slabplanet_aqua look unchanged, but slabplanet and slabplanet_terra have changes
- [ ] rename abstract types (e.g. `LandModelSimulation` -> `AbstractLandSimulation`) to distinguish from constructors [do this in a separate PR to reduce diff] - see https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/pull/1688
